title: American History—Memory Game
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: This American History Memory Game will teach children (primarily ages 5-10) names and images of important names and places from American history. Patterned after the vintage card game of Concentration, students try to find matching tiles. The one who finds the most matching tiles, wins! (Some students younger than 5 will enjoy this one because the thick tiles are easier to pick up than cards.) Easy, fun, and informative, this memory game is an author favorite!

title: FREE “Building Your Best Home Library” PDF
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: About the podcast: If you teach at home, you’re probably on the endless quest to build your best home library—a worthy endeavor that’s not without challenges! While ideas will vary on how to build your library, the chances are we agree on why. Join me and my guest, Nicki Truesdell of Knowledge Keepers, for the importance of building a home library (big or small), as well as ways to save, organize, and display your favorite books. About the PDF: This PDF is free to print for The Mystery of Home Education podcast listeners. It’s a list of some of Linda and Nicki’s home library favorites and the categories they fit under.

title: FREE “Kids and Cuss Words” PDF
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: About the podcast: Home educated children are not immune to hearing bad words—and repeating them. Profanity is everywhere and in every culture around the world! How then do we deal with kids and cuss words? Opinions will vary, but you can join Linda Lacour Hobar, author of The Mystery of History and veteran homeschool mom, for a proactive strategy that may help. As a gift to your children, address this issue ahead of time—with God’s Word as a guide. (Don’t miss Linda’s free printable template to use with your children.) About the PDF: This PDF is free to print for The Mystery of Home Education podcast listeners.

title: FREE “The Mystery of History Guide to Newbery Medal Winners” PDF
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: Since 1922, the American Library Association has awarded only one book per year the distinguished Newbery Medal —designed to recognize outstanding American literature for children. As a guide to parents and teachers, The Mystery of History has condensed the Newbery Medal list of winners to the categories of “historical fiction” and “biographies” that correlate well with our world history curriculum. This PDF is free to print for The Mystery of Home Education podcast listeners.

grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: For a concise look at Marxism and its historical roots, join Linda Lacour Hobar for “What is Marxism?” This PDF worksheet and answer key is free to print for The Mystery of Home Education podcast listeners.

Each chapter offers meaty discussion questions, Biblical truths with application, and memory verses. Need additional study guides for multiple students? You can buy them individually here.

title: Legends of King Arthur and His Court, revised by Shelley Noonan
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: Discover the world of kings, knights, and adventure! “These stories of King Arthur are retold in the faith that chivalry in its highest sense is not dead among us; and that as long as Christian manhood survives, so will chivalric instinct be an important factor in it.” (Francis Nimmo Greene). Throughout the pages of this book, readers will follow Arthur from his birth to the fall of his beloved Camelot. Whether man or myth, Arthur epitomizes good fighting against evil, light battling against darkness, and a hero who wants to make right that which is wrong! (92 pages). This purchase comes with a free digital Bible study, “Legacy of King Arthur and His Court” by Shelley Noonan—who explores ten godly character traits that the knights sought and struggled to uphold. Each chapter offers meaty discussion questions, Biblical truths with application, and memory verses. Want a matching audiobook narrated by Linda Lacour Hobar? We made it! Want a paperback study guide to go along? We have it! Want our King Arthur Bundle to save 15%? We have that, too!

description: The Mystery of History Volume III covers world history from the Wars of the Roses in England (1455) to Isaac Watts (1707). With the addition of our streaming video lectures*, students can go deeper! Linda Lacour Hobar, author of The Mystery of History , offers eighty-four 10-20 minute lectures (one per lesson) to further each topic of Volume III with slides, information, virtual field trips, interviews, and inspiration from a biblical worldview. (The lectures are not designed to replace the Student Reader , but to expand on it.) Professionally mastered, these videos may be enjoyed visually by the whole family, but the detail of the lectures is best suited for students 13 and up. *Due to file size, these lectures are accessed through a streaming membership on our website and are not downloadable. Membership is 18 months to allow families some stop and start flexibility!

title: Volume IV Planning Pages (PDF)
grades_or_ages: Grades 1-5; Volume I (Creation to A.D. 33); Grades 10-12; Volume IV (Wars of Independence to Modern Times)
description: These Planning Pages were designed to help families* line up our Volume IV Student Reader with our First or Second Edition Companion Guide** —in one easy-to-use 30-page planner! You can print these pages for a notebook, or use them in Adobe Reader with tools for text, sticky notes, highlighting, box checking, signing, etc. If you’re the scheduling type or not, these Planning Pages will help you “look ahead” to see and enjoy the many features of The Mystery of History.

title: Volume IV Self-paced Course (18-month Membership)
grades_or_ages: Grades 1-5; Volume I (Creation to A.D. 33); Grades 10-12; Volume IV (Wars of Independence to Modern Times)

title: Volume IV Video Lectures (18-month Membership)
grades_or_ages: Grades 1-5; Volume I (Creation to A.D. 33); Grades 10-12; Volume IV (Wars of Independence to Modern Times)
description: The Mystery of History Volume IV covers world history from Johann Sebastian Bach (1708) to the global war on terror (2001–2014). With the addition of our streaming video lectures*, students can go deeper! Linda Lacour Hobar, author of The Mystery of History , offers eighty-four 10-25 minute lectures (one per lesson) to further each topic of Volume IV with slides, information, virtual field trips, interviews, and inspiration from a biblical worldview. (The lectures are not designed to replace the Student Reader , but to expand on it.) Professionally mastered, these videos may be enjoyed visually by the whole family, but the detail of the lectures is best suited for students 13 and up. *Due to file size, these lectures are accessed through a streaming membership on our website and are not downloadable. Membership is 18 months to allow families some stop and start flexibility!

title: Volume IV—Audiobook (MP3)
grades_or_ages: Grades 1-5; Volume I (Creation to A.D. 33); Grades 10-12; Volume IV (Wars of Independence to Modern Times)
description: Bring The Mystery of History Volume IV to life with a word for word narration by the author. This listening library is sure to enhance your study of modern history. Be it for bedtime, class time, lunchtime, or a road trip, you can listen and learn! This product now comes with a printable “Listening Guide” (in color or black & white) to help you track your progress and see upcoming lessons and dates. (Total listening time: 27 hrs 11 min)

title: Volume IV—Challenge Cards (PDF)
grades_or_ages: Grades 1-5; Volume I (Creation to A.D. 33); Grades 10-12; Volume IV (Wars of Independence to Modern Times)
description: Designed by Bright Ideas Press, “Challenge Cards” are two-sided printable question-and-answer flashcards—capturing the most important elements in The Mystery of History Volume IV.

title: Volume IV—Coloring Pages (PDF)
grades_or_ages: Grades 1-5; Volume I (Creation to A.D. 33); Grades 10-12; Volume IV (Wars of Independence to Modern Times); All Ages
description: Students of all ages can enjoy these beautifully designed coloring pages made exclusively for The Mystery of History Volume IV. Different from Volume I, which offers a collage-style page, this 28-page set has three panels per page with each panel depicting a different lesson from the week.

title: Volume IV—Companion Guide
grades_or_ages: Grades 1-5; Volume I (Creation to A.D. 33); Grades 10-12; Volume IV (Wars of Independence to Modern Times); All Ages
description: All of our hardback volumes come with a one-time download code (in the cover) for a digital Companion Guide* loaded with curriculum for all ages! But, if the code has been used, this is the product you’re looking for. *Before you add this product to your cart, please consider the variables listed below (ie. Format, Edition.) This is a tricky point because in March 2025, Bright Ideas Press released a NEW Second Edition of our Volume IV Companion Guide! (Don’t be confused! The Student Reader is still in its First Edition; only the Companion Guide was updated.) The Second Edition Companion Guide offers you an improved format, updated activities, revised mapping exercises, new literature ideas, etc. We recommend the Second Edition UNLESS you’re in a group that is using the First Edition. The editions are different enough by page number, activity names, mapping exercises, etc. that participants will want to be in the same edition.

description: The Mystery of History Volume IV (retailing at $99.95) covers world history from Johann Sebastian Bach (1708) to the global war on terror (2001–2014). Where most modern history books focus heavily on the World Wars, The Mystery of History Volume IV weaves American history into the pages of world history for proper context—on one thought-provoking timeline. (Access to a digital Companion Guide, a 500+ page PDF resource—full of curriculum for all ages—is found on the inside cover of the book!) The ISBN of our current First Edition* is: 978-1892427304 .

title: WonderMaps (Downloadable PDF)
grades_or_ages: Grades 1-5; Volume I (Creation to A.D. 33); Grades 6-8; Volume II (The Early Church to the Renaissance)
description: WonderMaps is a customizable collection of over 350 digital maps. With nearly endless possibilities, WonderMaps makes it easy to integrate map study with The Mystery of History (Volumes III-IV.) ( WonderMaps is not yet compatible with Volume I, Third Edition or Volume II, Second Edition.)

title: “A Crash Course from Creation to Christ” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: Planning to teach ancient history? Need help integrating the Bible? Listen to Linda Lacour Hobar as she takes you from Creation to the birth of Christ in about one hour – shaping Biblical and secular history into one amazing timeline with portraits of King Tut, King David, Nebuchadnezzar, Buddha, Cleopatra, and more. Comes as an MP3 (audio only)

title: “Artists of the Renaissance that Everyone Ought to Know” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: Need to brush up on art history? Want a better grasp of the masters of the Renaissance? Join Linda Lacour Hobar for a delightful presentation of six major artists of the Renaissance that everyone ought to know. Your purchase includes a FREE PDF worksheet and answer key. Comes as an MP4 (video with slides)

title: “Bad Boys of the 20th Century!” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: Who are the worst of the worst dictators of the 20th century? Opinions may vary but you can join Linda Lacour Hobar to learn about the chilling rise to power of Lenin , Mussolini , Stalin , and Hitler . No previous knowledge is needed to appreciate what “should” be learned from history!

title: “From Paul to the Fall” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: From the road to Damascus to the fall of the Roman Empire, God has been at work to make Himself known. Follow Linda Lacour Hobar through the first five centuries of world history following the resurrection of Jesus Christ. Comes as an MP3 (audio only)

title: “Mary and Martha Decide to Homeschool” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: The presenter of this workshop is a self-proclaimed Mary (Linda Lacour Hobar, author of The Mystery of History ) who managed to homeschool for 17 years, and still likes to talk about it. (This workshop is a crowd favorite!)

title: “Modern History that Shook the World” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: With vivid snapshots of the Cold War , the Formation of Israel , the War in Korea , the Collapse of the Soviet Union , and more, students and teachers will not only see the past more clearly, but be better prepared for the future!

title: “The Collapse of the Soviet Union and Why it Matters” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: The collapse of the Soviet Union is one of the most unprecedented events in modern history! How did it happen and why does it matter? Join Linda Lacour Hobar, author of The Mystery of History, for a sobering look at the founding of the Soviet Union to its shocking early dissolution. This fact-filled session will inspire the study of world history in your homeschool. (Students are encouraged to listen in!) Comes as an MP3 (audio only) Length: Approximately 50 minutes

title: “The Dark Side of History” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: Does the dark side of history get you down? Fear not! According to Linda Lacour Hobar, the gloom and doom of dark world history can be a valuable and effective tool for teaching life lessons and reaching the hearts of your students with the Gospel. Your purchase includes a FREE PDF of Life Questions. Comes as an MP3 (audio only) or MP4 (video with slides)

title: “The Dark Side of Socialism” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: Polls suggest that over 50% of young Americans favor “socialism” but only 16% can properly define it! What is socialism? Join Linda Lacour Hobar for a historical overview of socialism and why it matters now! Your purchase includes a FREE PDF of Talking Points. Comes as an MP3 (audio only) or MP4 (video with slides)

title: “The Date-Free Lesson Planner” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: After years of frustration, veteran homeschooler Linda Lacour Hobar found practical ways to liberate her lesson plans and prevent anarchy in her home. Literally. The secret lies in her “Date-Free Lesson Planner” – a tool you will want to consider using in your homeschool to keep students and teacher in better harmony. (Planning sheets available!)

title: “The Good, the Bad, and the Ugly” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: Women, do you need to get real about homeschool and the joy and pain of life? Join Linda Lacour Hobar for this down-to-earth session on the up and down seasons of a real homeschool family. (Adults only please.) Comes as an MP4 (video with slides)

title: “The Holocaust: What Teens and ‘Tween Should Know” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: The history of the Holocaust is difficult to learn, difficult to grasp, and difficult to teach. But—to prevent it from happening again—it is history that demands our examination! Join Linda Lacour Hobar for a pensive look at the Holocaust and what teens and ‘tweens should know about it.

title: “The Mystery of History for All Ages” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages
description: Join Linda Lacour Hobar in discovering how to teach world history to the entire family. Using a “Chronological, Christian, Complete” approach, watch history come alive for younger, middle, and older students of all learning styles with hands-on activities, games, dramatizations, ethnic cooking, trivia contests, creative research, and much more. Comes as an MP3 (audio only)

title: “The Rise and Fall of Rome” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: Most of us have heard of the rise and fall of Rome. But we’re only vaguely familiar with who, what, when, and how this occurred—as well as why it matters! Join Linda Lacour Hobar for insight into the story of the western Roman empire….and how it applies to our world today. Comes as an MP4 (video with slides)

title: “The Threat of Socialism” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: An alarming number of U.S. millennials favor “socialism” as a means of government. What is socialism? Join Linda Lacour Hobar for a historical overview of socialism and why it matters now! (Teens and ‘tweens—don’t miss this one!) Comes as an MP3 (audio only)

title: “To Grade or Not to Grade” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: Shaped by our personality types, grades have the power to motivate some and the propensity to destroy others! So, how do you decide when to grade or not to grade? Join Linda Lacour Hobar for varying theories on grade keeping, tips for grading numerous subjects, and practical ideas for managing the grades you choose to keep. Comes as an MP3 (audio only) or MP4 (video with slides)

title: “Unleashing the Power of World History” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages
description: Through abounding stories of good and evil, world history has the power to befuddle our minds, disturb our souls, or inspire our hearts! Join Linda Lacour Hobar for an insightful look at unleashing the power of world history to stir hearts of all ages toward the truths of the Gospel. Comes as an MP3 (audio only)

title: “What Teens and Tweens Should Know about WWI” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: From trenches and truces on the Western Front to famous flying aces in the sky, let Linda Lacour Hobar walk you through an invigorating summary of what teens and ‘tweens should know about World War I through unforgettable stories and Linda’s exclusive “5-4-3-2-1 Countdown.” Your purchase includes a FREE PDF worksheet and answer key. Comes as an MP3 (audio only) or MP4 (video with slides)

title: “What Teens and ‘Tweens Should Know About the French Revolution” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: History is a great teacher. This is particularly true of the French Revolution when “good guys go bad!” Join Linda Lacour Hobar for blood-curdling, drama-filled, heart-wrenching events of the French Revolution that teens and tweens should know—and why these events matter today. Your purchase includes a FREE PDF worksheet and answer key. Comes as an MP4 (video with slides)

title: “When Bible History and World History Meet Face to Face” Workshop
grades_or_ages: All Ages; K-12 World History
description: Looking for inspiration in your study of ancient times? Join Linda Lacour Hobar to discover what happens when Bible history and world history meet face to face—literally! Your purchase includes a FREE PDF worksheet and answers key. Comes as an MP3 (audio only) or MP4 (video with slides)
